subject: ELA
title: Story Elements
description: Characters, setting, plot, conflict, and theme in fiction.
passage:
The Lost Kite

On a breezy Saturday morning, Maya and her brother Luca ran to the park to fly their new kite. It was bright red with a golden tail and had taken them all week to build. They laughed as it climbed higher and higher into the blue sky.

Suddenly, a strong gust of wind yanked the string out of Luca's hand. The kite soared over the trees and got tangled in the highest branch of an old oak tree. Maya's heart sank.

'We'll never get it down,' Luca said, his voice full of disappointment.

Maya looked around. She spotted Mr. Chen, their neighbour, trimming his garden nearby. She walked over and explained what happened. Mr. Chen smiled kindly. 'I have a ladder in my garage,' he said. 'Let's take a look.'

With Mr. Chen's help, they carefully freed the kite from the branches. It had a small tear, but Luca patched it up with tape from his pocket. By noon, the kite was flying again.

That evening, Maya wrote in her journal: 'Today I learned that asking for help is not a sign of weakness — it is a sign of wisdom.'

questions.q: Who are the MAIN characters in this story?
questions.explanation: All three play important roles in the story.

questions.q: Where does most of the story take place? (two words)
questions.explanation: The story begins and mostly takes place at the park.

questions.q: What is the main CONFLICT in the story?
options: The kite gets stuck in a tree
questions.answer: The kite gets stuck in a tree
questions.explanation: The central problem is the kite getting tangled in the oak tree.

options: Mr. Chen uses a ladder to free the kite
questions.answer: Mr. Chen uses a ladder to free the kite
questions.explanation: Mr. Chen brings a ladder and helps free the kite from the tree.

questions.q: What is the THEME (lesson) of the story?
options: Never fly kites on windy days
Asking for help is a sign of wisdom
questions.answer: Asking for help is a sign of wisdom
questions.explanation: Maya writes this lesson in her journal at the very end.

questions.q: What character TRAIT best describes Maya?
questions.explanation: Maya looks for a solution and bravely asks Mr. Chen for help.

questions.q: What did Luca use to fix the tear in the kite?
questions.explanation: 'Luca patched it up with tape from his pocket.'

questions.q: What is the SETTING (time) of this story?
questions.explanation: 'On a breezy Saturday morning' — as stated in the first line.

questions.q: What does the word DISAPPOINTMENT mean in this story?
options: Sadness about something not going as hoped
questions.answer: Sadness about something not going as hoped
questions.explanation: Luca's voice was 'full of disappointment' after the kite got stuck — he was sad.

questions.q: This story is told from which POINT OF VIEW?
questions.explanation: The narrator uses 'Maya', 'Luca', 'her', 'his' — third person perspective.
